trade.summary: Bilateral trade is substantial and growing. India exports diamonds (rough), chemicals, textiles, and pharmaceuticals. Israel exports defence equipment, diamonds (polished), chemicals, and agricultural technology. The India-Israel bilateral trade relationship is anchored by defence and high-technology cooperation. A bilateral FTA has been under negotiation.

diplomatic.summary: India and Israel established full diplomatic relations in 1992. Since then, ties have deepened significantly especially under Modi, who made the first visit by an Indian PM to Israel in 2017. Relations are described as a 'strategic partnership'. India has generally abstained on UN votes criticizing Israel, reflecting a careful balancing act between its pro-Palestinian historical stance and growing strategic ties with Israel. The Gaza war post-2023 created pressure on India to take a clearer position.
recent_incidents: India abstained on several UN resolutions on Gaza (2023-2025)
India called for restraint and humanitarian corridors in Gaza

military.summary: Defence is the cornerstone of India-Israel relations. Israel is India's second-largest arms supplier (after Russia). Major systems include Barak air defence, Heron drones, Spike anti-tank missiles, and naval systems. Joint R&D through DRDO-Israel defence cooperation. The relationship is highly institutionalized in defence procurement.

financial.summary: Israeli tech companies have significant presence in India. Indian IT companies have offices in Israel. Israeli VC funds invest in Indian startups. Bilateral FDI flows have grown. The two countries are exploring a bilateral investment treaty.

energy.summary: Limited bilateral energy trade historically. Israel's Leviathan gas field raises possibilities for LNG exports to India. India has expressed interest in Israeli water desalination and solar technology.

scientific.summary: Strong scientific and technological cooperation, especially in agriculture, water management, and cybersecurity. The India-Israel Industrial R&D Fund (ISERD) supports joint research. Agricultural cooperation (drip irrigation, precision farming) has benefited India's farming sector.
